vertical: Supplier compliance & contractor safety SaaS — industrial enterprise (oil & gas, utilities, construction)
campaign_name: Tier-2 Supplier Re-Engagement — Compliance Lapse → Renewal
objective: Reactivate Tier-2 supplier accounts whose compliance verification lapsed in the last 90 days, route engaged accounts to AE-qualified pipeline, and recover ~12% of dormant ARR within one quarter.

segment.name: Lapsed Tier-2 Suppliers · No Login 90d · Active Hiring Client
salesforce_filters: Contact.Role IN ('EHS Manager', 'Safety Director', 'Procurement Lead', 'Compliance Officer')
exclusions: Account on AE-active pipeline within last 14 days (suppression: pipeline conflict)
segment.estimated_size: ~2,400 contacts across ~840 accounts (38% of total Tier-2 lapsed cohort, post-suppression)

touches.subject: Your {{Account.Industry}} clients are still requiring verified suppliers
touches.body_brief: Plain-text feel, no graphics. Lead with the contractual reality: their hiring clients (named via Salesforce relationship) require verified suppliers per active contracts. Soft CTA: 'See what's expired' → deep link to their compliance dashboard with auto-login token. No discount language. Sign from named CSM, not generic.
qa_rules: Reject if any token is null or 'N/A' (re-route to fallback variant without tokens)
Reject if subject line >65 chars (mobile preview truncation)
Reject if body contains regulated terms ('guarantee', 'warranty', 'OSHA-approved') — legal-flagged in past audit

touches.timing: T+0 (fires on next login, persists 7 days)
touches.subject: Compliance status: action required
touches.body_brief: Sticky banner on dashboard load. Shows: number of expired docs, named hiring clients affected, single CTA 'Renew now (3 min)'. Dismissible but re-fires after 48h if not actioned. Never fires for users with active support cases.
qa_rules: Render fallback static text if hiring-client list >5 entries (UI overflow)

touches.timing: T+5 days (skip if step 1 or 2 converted)
touches.subject: Variant A: '{{Account.HiringClient_TopName}} flagged your account' / Variant B: 'Quick renewal — 3 minutes, no calls'
touches.body_brief: Optimization agent owns variant selection: bandit allocation across two subject lines and two body framings (consequence-led vs effort-led). Body cites the specific hiring-client contract clause requiring active verification. Includes one 30-sec Loom from a CSM if account.ARR >$25K (deterministic threshold, not LLM-decided).
qa_rules: Reject if account in active opportunity stage 'Negotiation' or later (sales conflict)
Reject Variant A if hiring-client name not legally permissible to disclose in marketing (legal-list check)
Bandit floor: minimum 200 sends per variant before reallocation (avoid early bias)
touches.success_metric: Open rate >42% (post-segmentation effect); reply rate >2.5% (proxy for AE handoff signal)

touches.timing: T+7 days (fires only if engagement score >= threshold)
touches.subject: AE task: account {{Account.Name}} qualified for outbound
touches.body_brief: QA agent inspects engagement signals across steps 1–3 (opens, banner CTR, page visits, in-app actions). If composite score >= 0.62, creates Salesforce task for AE assigned to the territory with: engagement timeline, hiring-client contract context, suggested talk track. AE accepts or rejects task within 48h or it auto-routes to pooled queue. QA agent never sends outbound itself — only flags.
qa_rules: Score threshold (0.62) is feature-flagged — adjust via campaign config, not code
Suppress if AE.OOO__c = TRUE → route to backup AE in same territory
Reject task creation if account already has open task <14 days old (deduplication)
touches.success_metric: Task acceptance rate >70%; AE-touched accounts → renewal close rate +18% vs control

touches.timing: T+14 days (final touch, suppressed for any prior conversion or AE-claimed account)
touches.subject: Last note before we pause outreach — {{Account.Name}}
touches.body_brief: Permission-based exit. Three options inline: (1) renew now, (2) tell us why you paused, (3) opt out of compliance reminders. Option 2 routes to a 1-question form (not a 'book a meeting' link — friction kills the signal). The exit framing measurably outperforms 'final reminder' framing in our A/B history (cite internal benchmark in agent prompt, not in email body).
qa_rules: Hard suppression if any conversion event in last 14 days
Hard suppression if AE has logged a 'Do Not Email' note in the last 30 days
Reject if subject line A/B variants haven't been pre-approved by brand QA queue
touches.success_metric: Reply rate >4% (Option 2 is the highest-value response); unsub <1.2% (acceptable; this is the exit touch)

optimization_triggers.action: suppress entire campaign and route to CSM
CSM owns motion, marketing fully suppressed

optimization_triggers.condition: Industry='Construction' segment open rate <25% after 14 days
optimization_triggers.action: optimization agent re-segments by sub-vertical (residential vs commercial vs civil)

pipeline_projection.expected_renewals: ~$1.4M ARR recovered (12% of $11.6M lapsed Tier-2 cohort, blended close rate 14% on AE-touched, 4% on self-serve renewal)
pipeline_projection.ae_efficiency: ~70% of created tasks expected to convert vs ~22% on cold outbound — CAC reduction proxy
pipeline_projection.campaign_runtime: 21 days end-to-end; reporting deck auto-generated by optimization agent at T+30
pipeline_projection.downside: If hiring-client name disclosure fails legal review for >40% of segment, pipeline projection drops ~30% (Variant B fallback compresses lift)

meta.platform_fit: Salesforce two-way sync (account/contact filters, AE task creation), warehouse sync (product engagement, intent), real-time segmentation (suppression rules, dedup), three AI agents (execution, QA, optimization) with non-overlapping responsibilities, no-code orchestration of touch sequencing and triggers.
meta.build_time_on_conversion_platform: ~3 hours from blank canvas, assuming Salesforce + Snowflake already connected. The brief shape mirrors what the platform consumes — segment, sequence, agent assignments, QA rules, triggers — so this document is a config, not a doc.
meta.what_this_proves: I've shipped multi-agent orchestration in production at 0–2 YOE. The shape of the work is identical: signals → segmentation → agent assignment → execution with QA → optimization. The funnel stage is different (prospecting vs MarOps), the engine is the same.
